propositions: Add opt-in config keys identity.require_authenticated (default off) and identity.signing_key so a project can enforce authenticated authorship.

Optionally sign each event's canonical bytes (excluding author_sig) at write time in append_event, storing a DSSE envelope as author_sig; signing failures fall back to writing unsigned and never break the write.
A UX-only write-gate fails fast when require_authenticated is on and a write cannot be signed, but this is explicitly not the security boundary.

The verify-authorship merge-gate command verifies each mutating event and flags it verified/unsigned/unknown-author/bad-signature, exiting non-zero when require_authenticated is on and any event is not verified, else advisory exit 0.
Replay never rejects: bad-signature or unsigned events still fold into compiled state, and the reducer records only per-ticket signed/unsigned presence counts, surfaced by show and fsck.
Compaction preserves each folded event's author_id and author_sig into a snapshot authorship_ledger so verify-authorship still works after raw event files are gone.
